title: Verbs - Action Words
subject: Language Arts
description: Learn about action words (verbs), past tense, irregular verbs, and helping verbs like is/are, has/have, was/were, do/does.
curriculum: Jamaica NSC Grade 2 - Language Arts: Grammar & Conventions
nscObjectives: Recognize irregular verbs that don't take -ed
Use is/are correctly with singular and plural subjects
Use has/have correctly with singular and plural subjects
Use was/were correctly for past tense

sections.name: Past Tense with -ed
sections.instruction: To make a verb past tense, we usually add -ed to the end. The -ed can sound like 't', 'd', or 'id'.

questions.prompt: What is the past tense of 'push'?
questions.hint: Add -ed to make it past tense.

questions.prompt: What is the past tense of 'jump'?
questions.hint: Add -ed to make it past tense.

questions.prompt: What is the past tense of 'finish'?
questions.hint: Add -ed to make it past tense.

questions.prompt: What is the past tense of 'bowl'?
questions.hint: When a verb ends with a vowel sound, adding -ed makes a 'd' sound.

questions.prompt: What is the past tense of 'clean'?
questions.hint: When a verb ends with a vowel sound, adding -ed makes a 'd' sound.

questions.prompt: What is the past tense of 'climb'?
questions.hint: Add -ed to make it past tense.

questions.prompt: What is the past tense of 'play'?
questions.hint: Words ending with 'ay' have a vowel sound. Adding -ed makes a 'd' sound.

questions.prompt: Which sound does -ed make in 'pushed'?
questions.hint: After p, k, f, s, sh, ch, and th, -ed sounds like 't'.

questions.prompt: Which sound does -ed make in 'cleaned'?
questions.hint: After vowel sounds and voiced consonants, -ed sounds like 'd'.

questions.prompt: Which sound does -ed make in 'shouted'?
questions.hint: After 't' or 'd', -ed sounds like 'id'.

sections.name: Past Tense Special Rules
sections.instruction: Some verbs have special rules: words ending in 'e' just add 'd', words ending in 'y' change to 'ied', and short words double the last letter.

questions.prompt: What is the past tense of 'stare'? (ends in 'e')
questions.hint: Words ending with 'e' just add 'd' for past tense.

questions.prompt: What is the past tense of 'smile'? (ends in 'e')
questions.hint: Words ending with 'e' just add 'd' for past tense.

questions.prompt: What is the past tense of 'love'? (ends in 'e')
questions.hint: Words ending with 'e' just add 'd' for past tense.

questions.prompt: What is the past tense of 'carry'? (ends in 'y')
questions.hint: Change 'y' to 'i' before adding -ed.

questions.prompt: What is the past tense of 'cry'? (ends in 'y')
questions.hint: Change 'y' to 'i' before adding -ed.

questions.prompt: What is the past tense of 'hurry'? (ends in 'y')
questions.hint: Change 'y' to 'i' before adding -ed.

questions.prompt: What is the past tense of 'clap'? (short word)
questions.hint: Double the last letter before adding -ed.

questions.prompt: What is the past tense of 'stop'? (short word)
questions.hint: Double the last letter before adding -ed.

questions.prompt: What is the past tense of 'hop'? (short word)
questions.hint: Double the last letter before adding -ed.

questions.prompt: What is the past tense of 'drop'? (short word)
questions.hint: Double the last letter before adding -ed.

sections.name: Irregular Verbs
sections.instruction: Some verbs do NOT take -ed to form their past tense. These are called irregular verbs. You need to learn them!

questions.prompt: Some verbs do NOT take -ed to form their past tense. These are called:
questions.hint: These verbs are not regular - they follow different rules.

questions.prompt: What is the past tense of 'run'?
questions.hint: This is an irregular verb - it doesn't add -ed.

questions.prompt: What is the past tense of 'come'?
questions.hint: This is an irregular verb - it doesn't add -ed.

questions.prompt: What is the past tense of 'eat'?
questions.hint: This is an irregular verb - it doesn't add -ed.

questions.prompt: What is the past tense of 'drink'?
questions.hint: This is an irregular verb - it doesn't add -ed.

questions.prompt: What is the past tense of 'sleep'?
questions.hint: This is an irregular verb - it doesn't add -ed.

questions.prompt: What is the past tense of 'write'?
questions.hint: This is an irregular verb - it doesn't add -ed.

questions.prompt: What is the past tense of 'buy'?
questions.hint: This is an irregular verb - it doesn't add -ed.

questions.prompt: What is the past tense of 'give'?
questions.hint: This is an irregular verb - it doesn't add -ed.

questions.prompt: What is the past tense of 'take'?
questions.hint: This is an irregular verb - it doesn't add -ed.

questions.prompt: What is the past tense of 'see'?
questions.hint: This is an irregular verb - it doesn't add -ed.

questions.prompt: What is the past tense of 'fly'?
questions.hint: This is an irregular verb - it doesn't add -ed.

questions.prompt: What is the past tense of 'go'?
questions.hint: This is an irregular verb - it doesn't add -ed.

questions.prompt: What is the past tense of 'drive'?
questions.hint: This is an irregular verb - it doesn't add -ed.

questions.prompt: What is the past tense of 'sing'?
questions.hint: This is an irregular verb - it doesn't add -ed.

sections.name: Is and Are
sections.instruction: Use 'is' when talking about ONE person, place, animal, or thing. Use 'are' when talking about MORE THAN ONE. Always use 'are' with 'you'.

questions.prompt: The boy ___ in the tree. (is/are)
questions.hint: Use 'is' when talking about ONE person.

questions.prompt: The boys ___ in the tree. (is/are)
questions.hint: Use 'are' when talking about MORE THAN ONE person.

questions.prompt: The apple ___ on the table. (is/are)
questions.hint: Use 'is' when talking about ONE thing.

questions.prompt: The apples ___ on the table. (is/are)
questions.hint: Use 'are' when talking about MORE THAN ONE thing.

questions.prompt: You ___ late for school. (is/are)
questions.hint: Always use 'are' with the word 'you'.

questions.prompt: The children ___ reading. (is/are)
questions.hint: Children is more than one person, so use 'are'.

questions.prompt: The box ___ blue. (is/are)
questions.hint: One box = use 'is'.

questions.prompt: The boxes ___ blue. (is/are)
questions.hint: More than one box = use 'are'.

questions.prompt: We ___ going to get the whistle. (is/are)
questions.hint: 'We' means more than one person.

questions.prompt: The dog ___ eating bones. (is/are)
questions.hint: One dog = use 'is'.

sections.name: Has and Have
sections.instruction: Use 'has' when talking about ONE person, place, animal, or thing. Use 'have' when talking about MORE THAN ONE. Always use 'have' with 'I' and 'you'.

questions.prompt: Mother ___ a new baby. (has/have)
questions.hint: Use 'has' when talking about ONE person.

questions.prompt: Our mothers ___ new babies. (has/have)
questions.hint: Use 'have' when talking about MORE THAN ONE person.

questions.prompt: The goose ___ a long neck. (has/have)
questions.hint: One goose = use 'has'.

questions.prompt: The geese ___ long necks. (has/have)
questions.hint: Geese is more than one goose = use 'have'.

questions.prompt: I ___ a big umbrella. (has/have)
questions.hint: Always use 'have' with 'I' and 'you'.

questions.prompt: You ___ a pretty dress. (has/have)
questions.hint: Always use 'have' with 'I' and 'you'.

questions.prompt: The doctor ___ the stethoscope. (has/have)
questions.hint: One doctor = use 'has'.

questions.prompt: The pencils ___ sharp points. (has/have)
questions.hint: More than one pencil = use 'have'.

questions.prompt: Uncle Stephen ___ a chicken farm. (has/have)
questions.hint: One person = use 'has'.

questions.prompt: The teachers ___ some books on the table. (has/have)
questions.hint: More than one teacher = use 'have'.

sections.name: Was and Were
sections.instruction: Use 'was' and 'were' to tell about something that happened in the past. Use 'was' for ONE, 'were' for MORE THAN ONE. Use 'were' with 'you'.

questions.prompt: The cup ___ on the table. (was/were)
questions.hint: Use 'was' when talking about ONE thing in the past.

questions.prompt: The cups ___ on the table. (was/were)
questions.hint: Use 'were' when talking about MORE THAN ONE in the past.

questions.prompt: The boy ___ climbing the tree. (was/were)
questions.hint: One boy = use 'was'.

questions.prompt: They ___ playing cricket. (was/were)
questions.hint: 'They' means more than one person = use 'were'.

questions.prompt: I ___ playing with my doll. (was/were)
questions.hint: Use 'was' with 'I' when talking about the past.

questions.prompt: You ___ not at the concert. (was/were)
questions.hint: Use 'were' with the word 'you'.

questions.prompt: The animals ___ taken from the zoo. (was/were)
questions.hint: More than one animal = use 'were'.

questions.prompt: The rain ___ falling. (was/were)
questions.hint: Rain is one thing = use 'was'.

questions.prompt: Many children ___ at the concert. (was/were)
questions.hint: Many children = more than one = use 'were'.

questions.prompt: It ___ not locked. (was/were)
questions.hint: 'It' refers to one thing = use 'was'.

sections.name: Do and Does
sections.instruction: Use 'does' when talking about ONE person or thing. Use 'do' when talking about MORE THAN ONE. Use 'do' with 'you' and 'I'.

questions.prompt: ___ Paul like to go to the library? (Do/Does)
questions.hint: Paul is one person = use 'Does'.

questions.prompt: ___ you like to walk alone? (Do/Does)
questions.hint: Use 'do' with the words 'you' and 'I'.

questions.prompt: We ___ our work everyday. (do/does)
questions.hint: 'We' means more than one person = use 'do'.

questions.prompt: She ___ not have to do it. (do/does)
questions.hint: 'She' is one person = use 'does'.

questions.prompt: ___ it look like a good day for the beach? (Do/Does)
questions.hint: 'It' is one thing = use 'Does'.

questions.prompt: What ___ your family do for recreation? (do/does)
questions.hint: Family is treated as one group = use 'does'.

questions.prompt: The children ___ well on the test. (do/does)
questions.hint: Children is more than one = use 'do'.

questions.prompt: The boy ___ his work well. (do/does)
questions.hint: One boy = use 'does'.

questions.prompt: I ___ not go to church on Mondays. (do/does)
questions.hint: Use 'do' with 'I'.

questions.prompt: The door ___ not have a lock. (do/does)
questions.hint: One door = use 'does'.

sections.name: Did Not
sections.instruction: Use 'did not' with a PRESENT TENSE verb, not a past tense verb. For example: 'I did not take' (not 'I did not took').

questions.prompt: She did not (drop/dropped) the money in the box.
questions.hint: Use 'did not' with a present tense verb.

questions.prompt: The children did not (tell/told) the truth.
questions.hint: Use 'did not' with a present tense verb.

questions.prompt: Mother did not (take/took) her purse with her.
questions.hint: Use 'did not' with a present tense verb.

questions.prompt: We did not (see/saw) the doctors at the hospital.
questions.hint: Use 'did not' with a present tense verb.

questions.prompt: He did not (buy/bought) the food from the shop.
questions.hint: Use 'did not' with a present tense verb.

questions.prompt: She did not (play/played) in the garden.
questions.hint: Use 'did not' with a present tense verb.

questions.prompt: They did not (hear/heard) the new principal.
questions.hint: Use 'did not' with a present tense verb.

questions.prompt: You did not (walk/walked) to the park.
questions.hint: Use 'did not' with a present tense verb.

questions.prompt: How many dogs did not (eat/ate) the bone?
questions.hint: Use 'did not' with a present tense verb.

questions.prompt: Charley did not (take/took) his bag to school.
questions.hint: Use 'did not' with a present tense verb.

sections.name: Adding -ing to Verbs
sections.instruction: Add -ing to verbs to show something is happening now. Some verbs have special rules: drop 'e' before -ing, double the last letter for short words.

questions.prompt: What is the -ing form of 'call'?
questions.hint: Just add -ing to the verb.

questions.prompt: What is the -ing form of 'push'?
questions.hint: Just add -ing to the verb.

questions.prompt: What is the -ing form of 'run'?
questions.hint: Double the last letter before adding -ing.

questions.prompt: What is the -ing form of 'swim'?
questions.hint: Double the last letter before adding -ing.

questions.prompt: What is the -ing form of 'dance'?
questions.hint: Drop the 'e' before adding -ing.

questions.prompt: What is the -ing form of 'smile'?
questions.hint: Drop the 'e' before adding -ing.

questions.prompt: What is the -ing form of 'worry'?
questions.hint: Just add -ing (don't change the 'y').

questions.prompt: What is the -ing form of 'play'?
questions.hint: Just add -ing to the verb.

questions.prompt: While Noah preached, he was (built/building) an ark.
questions.hint: Use the -ing form with 'was'.

questions.prompt: She is (wearing/wear) a pretty dress.
questions.hint: Use the -ing form with 'is'.
